results.possibleIdentity: Robert Martin Haralick (born 1943) is a distinguished engineer and computer scientist known for his pioneering work in computer vision and pattern recognition. In the mid-to-late 1960s, he was based in Lawrence, Kansas, where he attended the University of Kansas, receiving his B.A. in Mathematics (1964), B.S. (1966), M.S. (1967), and Ph.D. (1969) in Electrical Engineering. During this period, he served as a Research Engineer at the University of Kansas Center for Research, Inc., starting in 1965, and later joined the faculty of the Department of Electrical Engineering in 1969. His technical expertise includes cybernetics, image analysis, and multi-image pattern recognition; he is famously associated with the 'Haralick features' for image texture analysis. His profile matches records of engineers who participated in the Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.) technical services program, which utilized index cards to match scientists and engineers with artists for collaborative projects. Haralick eventually held prestigious positions at Virginia Tech, the University of Washington (as the Boeing Clairmont Egtvedt Professor), and the City University of New York (CUNY).
